meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Vocabulary Set 5
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Challenge set — antonyms, word families, advanced usage across all 16 words.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'equal'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'equal' (the same amount) is 'unequal' (different amounts). The monkey made sure the pieces were unequal so he could eat more.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'mistake'?
questions.explanation: A 'mistake' is something wrong or incorrect. The opposite is doing the 'right' or 'correct' thing. If you get the answer right, you made no mistake.

questions.question: 'Divide,' 'division,' and 'divided' all come from the same word family.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Divide' (verb), 'division' (noun), and 'divided' (past tense) are all from the same word family. They all relate to splitting things into parts.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'afraid'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'afraid' (scared) is 'brave' or 'unafraid' (not scared). A brave person is not afraid even when things are difficult.

questions.question: Match each word to its OPPOSITE.
questions.explanation: Opposites: tricky ↔ easy, sadly ↔ happily, carefully ↔ carelessly, fairly ↔ unfairly. Knowing antonyms helps you understand word meanings more deeply.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'fortune' and 'mistake' BOTH correctly?
options: It was a fortune mistake to enter the jungle.
Her fortune was to make a mistake — so lucky!
By good fortune, she found the mistake in her work before the teacher saw it.
The mistake was a great fortune for the cats.
questions.explanation: 'By good fortune' (luckily) she found the 'mistake' (error) before the teacher. Both words are used with their correct meanings.

questions.question: 'Care,' 'careful,' and 'carefully' are all related — 'care' is the root word.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Care' (noun/verb) → 'careful' (adjective) → 'carefully' (adverb). Learning the root word 'care' helps you understand the whole word family.

questions.question: The monkey pretended to be sorry, but he did not _______ feel bad about eating the cheese.
questions.hint: He was only pretending — not _______ sorry
questions.explanation: The monkey's apology was fake. He did not 'truly' (really, actually) feel bad. This is the point of the story — the monkey used words to trick the cats.

questions.question: In the story, the monkey behaved 'unfairly.' Which BEST describes what this means?
options: He divided the cheese into exactly equal pieces
He helped the cats as much as he could
He cheated by eating more cheese than the cats received
He refused to touch the cheese
questions.explanation: Acting 'unfairly' means not giving everyone an equal share. The monkey ate more cheese himself while pretending to help — that is unfair.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a sentence about the monkey's personality:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The cats could not believe how tricky the monkey was.' This captures both 'believe' and 'tricky' in a single meaningful sentence about the story's theme.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they BASE WORDS or WORD FORMS (changed versions)?
questions.explanation: 'Divide,' 'sad,' and 'care' are base/root words. 'Divided' (past tense), 'sadly' (adverb from sad), and 'carefully' (adverb from care) are changed forms.

questions.question: The sentence 'She swung the cheese exactly and fairly' uses these words correctly.
questions.explanation: No — 'swung' is used incorrectly. You 'swing' on a branch or swing set, not cheese. 'Exactly' and 'fairly' are fine as adverbs, but 'swung the cheese' doesn't make sense.

questions.question: Which TWO words from the story describe the SAME type of character trait?
options: 'Tricky' and 'exactly' — both are about being clever
'Tricky' describes the monkey's dishonesty; 'fairly' describes the OPPOSITE — honesty
questions.explanation: 'Tricky' = sneaky, dishonest. 'Fairly' = just, honest. They are OPPOSITES in meaning. The monkey claimed to act 'fairly' but was actually being 'tricky.' Understanding these contrasts reveals the story's point.

questions.question: It shows _______ good _______ to find the lost wallet and return it to its owner.
questions.explanation: 'Good fortune' means great luck. Finding a lost wallet and returning it is lucky for the owner — and finding it in the first place is the finder's good fortune.

questions.question: The monkey's trick worked because the cats were 'tricky' and pretended not to notice.
questions.explanation: No — the MONKEY was tricky, not the cats. The cats were honest and trusting, which is why the monkey's trickery worked. The cats genuinely did not realize they were being cheated.
